paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies graphene/MnPSe3 heterostructures to probe how an antiferromagnetic insulator affects graphene transport. The main finding is that MnPSe3 primarily p-dopes graphene through interfacial charge transfer, while its magnetic order does not measurably alter the quantum Hall states; however, a low-temperature resistance minimum around 20–40 K suggests an indirect magnetic contribution, plausibly via a Kondo-like mechanism rather than a direct antiferromagnetic phase-transition effect.

figures.description: XRD pattern of MnPSe3 powder compared with the standard MnPSe3 reference pattern; inset shows a grown MnPSe3 crystal.
Optical image of an exfoliated MnPSe3 flake and its Raman spectrum with labeled characteristic peaks P1–P5.
Temperature dependence of magnetic susceptibility of MnPSe3 single crystal under 1000 Oe for H//ab and H//c, used to identify magnetic anisotropy and Neel temperature.
Optical image of the graphene device before and after being covered by MnPSe3.
Longitudinal resistance versus gate voltage for graphene and graphene/MnPSe3 devices at 300 K, showing a Dirac-point shift after MnPSe3 transfer.
Raman spectra of graphene before and after MnPSe3 transfer, showing red shifts of G and 2D peaks.
Scatter plot of graphene G-peak and 2D-peak positions at multiple locations of the heterojunction, compared with strain and doping reference lines.
figures.data_type: scatter plot / extracted spectral peak positions

figures.description: Schematic band alignment before and after contact between graphene and MnPSe3, including work functions and band gap.
Quantum oscillations and quantum Hall effect at Vg = -50 V for different field-cooling magnetic fields, showing no drift.
Quantum Hall effect at Vg = -50 V for different field-cooling magnetic fields, showing no significant change.
Quantum oscillations at Vg = 60 V for different field-cooling magnetic fields, showing a small drift of about 100–300 mT.
Quantum Hall effect at Vg = 60 V for different field-cooling magnetic fields, showing no significant effect.
Temperature dependence of longitudinal resistance at Vg = 0 V, with magnetic susceptibility shown on the right axis.
Temperature dependence of longitudinal resistance at Vg = 60 V, with magnetic susceptibility shown on the right axis.
Schematic magnetic structure of MnPSe3 with indicated impurity magnetic moments.
Fitting and normalization of the resistance-temperature curves from four devices using Kondo theory, comparing experimental data with the Kondo model prediction.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include XRD patterns of MnPSe3 powder, Raman spectra of MnPSe3 and graphene before/after transfer, magnetic susceptibility versus temperature under 1000 Oe for H//ab and H//c, gate-dependent two-terminal and four-terminal resistance of graphene devices, magnetic-field-dependent longitudinal and Hall resistance for quantum Hall measurements under different field-cooling protocols, and temperature-dependent resistance curves for multiple devices.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The provided text does not include a formal data availability statement, repository link, or explicit mention of downloadable source data or code. It only states that a complete data set for D2 can be found in the supplemental material.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only implicitly through the measurement methods and figures; no file formats, repository names, or instrument export formats are given. Instruments mentioned include a VSM (Quantum Design), Raman spectroscopy with 488 nm excitation, XRD, and transport measurements on graphene devices.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted Dirac-point shift/doping level, Raman peak positions (Pos G and Pos 2D) and their shifts, susceptibility-derived Neel temperature, and Kondo-model fits to normalized R-T curves with extracted Kondo temperature TK. The paper also discusses normalization/fitting of resistance curves in Fig. 5b.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly presented in the main figures, and the text explicitly says a complete data set for D2 is in the supplemental material. It is unknown whether the supplemental material contains raw traces, source data tables, or only plotted data.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper mixes direct experimental traces with derived quantities and model fits. The only explicit dataset-sharing statement is the mention of a complete data set for device D2 in the supplemental material. There is no evidence of an external open repository or of separately provided scripts. The likely dataset modalities are image files for optical micrographs, diffraction patterns, Raman spectra, magnetic susceptibility curves, and transport traces (R-Vg, R-B, R-T, Hall resistance).

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports multiple direct measurements: XRD, Raman spectra, magnetic susceptibility, and transport curves, all shown as figures and described as experimental results.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The text explicitly mentions a 'complete data set for D2' in the supplemental material and includes figure-ready extracted quantities such as Raman peak positions and Kondo fits.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “A complete data set for D2 can be found in the supplemental material.”; “Figure 5b highlights the resistance upturn behavior ... fitted using theories of the Kondo effect.”; “Combining all the results together, as shown in Figure 2c...”
